description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by five wide, metallic hoops. The wood appears weathered and textured, with visible grain and some dark stains, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The metallic bands are slightly tarnished and riveted, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the staves, with the top surface flush with the rim. One side of the barrel features a small, round bunghole, typically used for filling or emptying the contents. The overall look is robust and utilitarian, suitable for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban or industrial settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, bulging sides, and a small bunghole on one face. The surface is rustic and reinforced, ideal for storage or transport.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat wooden top and the full set of metal hoops, with no visible bunghole.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the weathered texture of the wood, no bunghole visible.
description_view_2: Side view with the small round bunghole visible on one stave, showing the functional aspect of the barrel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no bunghole, displaying the consistent banding and wood texture.
